chunks.text: CJDA 38 JUDGMENT SHEET LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 (Muhammad Shabbir etc. Vs. The State etc.) (C.S.R NO. 34-T of 2007) Date of hearing: 23.10.2014 Appellants by: Ch. Muhammad Anayat Ullah Cheema and Malik Muhammad Suleman Awan, Advocate Complainant by: Syed Imdad Hussain Hamdani, Advocated State by: Mr. Khurram Khan, DPG Sadaqat Ali Khan, J.: This single judgment shall dispose of Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 filed by Mohammad Shabbir, Mohammad Ameer (since died), Mohammad Imran, Mohammad Munir and Mohammad Hussain present appellants (against their convictions) and C.S.R No.34-T of 2007 sent by the learned trial court i.e. Special Judge Anti-Terrorism Court, Sargodha for confirmation of the death sentences of appellants stated above or otherwise as both the matters have arisen out of the same judgment dated 29.03.2007 passed by learned Special Judge, Anti- Terrorism Court, Sargodha according to which all the present appellants were convicted:- (i) “under section 302/149 PPC and sentenced to death each for committing the murder of Naeem Ullah deceased along with compensation Rs.1,00,000/- each payable to the legal heirs of the deceased and in default thereof further undergo six months S.I. (ii) under sections 302/149 PPC and sentenced to death each for committing the murder of Hafeezullah deceased along with compensation Rs.1,00,000/- each payable to the legal heirs of the deceased Hafeezullah and in default thereof further undergo Six months S.I. (iii) under sections 302/149 PPC and sentenced to death each for committing the murder of Hameedullah deceased along with compensation Rs.1,00,000/- each payable to the legal heirs of the deceased Hameedullah and in default thereof further undergo Six months S.I.

Khurram Khan, DPG Sadaqat Ali Khan, J.: This single judgment shall dispose of Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 filed by Mohammad Shabbir, Mohammad Ameer (since died), Mohammad Imran, Mohammad Munir and Mohammad Hussain present appellants (against their convictions) and C.S.R No.34-T of 2007 sent by the learned trial court i.e. Special Judge Anti-Terrorism Court, Sargodha for confirmation of the death sentences of appellants stated above or otherwise as both the matters have arisen out of the same judgment dated 29.03.2007 passed by learned Special Judge, Anti- Terrorism Court, Sargodha according to which all the present appellants were convicted:- (i) “under section 302/149 PPC and sentenced to death each for committing the murder of Naeem Ullah deceased along with compensation Rs.1,00,000/- each payable to the legal heirs of the deceased and in default thereof further undergo six months S.I. (ii) under sections 302/149 PPC and sentenced to death each for committing the murder of Hafeezullah deceased along with compensation Rs.1,00,000/- each payable to the legal heirs of the deceased Hafeezullah and in default thereof further undergo Six months S.I. (iii) under sections 302/149 PPC and sentenced to death each for committing the murder of Hameedullah deceased along with compensation Rs.1,00,000/- each payable to the legal heirs of the deceased Hameedullah and in default thereof further undergo Six months S.I. CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 2 (iv) they were also convicted under sections 324/149 PPC and sentenced to ten years R.I each for attempting to commit the Qatl-i-Amd of Said Rasool along with fine Rs.50,000/- each and in default thereof further undergo three months S.I. (v) under sections 337-A(i)/149 PPC and to pay Rs. 25,000/- each as ‘Daman’ for causing Shajjah-e-Khafifah to Said Rasool and in default thereof further undergo two years R.I. (vi) under sections 337-A(ii)/149 PPC and to pay Rs. 15,000/- each for causing Shajjah-e- Mudiah to Said Rasool as five percent of ‘Arsh’ and also undergo five years R.I as Tazir. (vii) under sections 337-F(i)/149 PPC and to pay Rs.25,000/- each as ‘Daman’ for causing Ghayr-Jaifah Damiyah to Said Rasool and also undergo one year R.I as Tazir. (viii) under sections 337-F(iii)/149 PPC and to pay Rs. 25,000/- each as ‘Daman’ for causing Ghayr-Jaifah Mutalamimah to Said Rasool and also undergo three years R.I as Tazir. (ix) under sections 337-F(vi)/149 PPC and to pay Rs. 25,000/- as ‘Daman’ for causing Ghayr-Jaifah Munaqqillah to Said Rasool and also undergo seven years R.I each as Tazir. (x) under sections 148/149 PPC and sentenced to undergo three years R.I each. (xi) under sections 7 (a) of the Anti-Terrorism Act 1997 and sentenced to death each for committing the act of terrorism and causing death of three persons along with fine Rs.50,000/- each and in default thereof further under three years S.I. (xii) under sections 7 (c) of the Anti-Terrorism Act 1997 read with section 149 PPC and sentenced to life imprisonment each for committing an act of terrorism by causing serious injuries to Said Rasool along with fine Rs.50,000/- each and in default thereof further under three years S.I. in case FIR No. 72 dated 16.2.2005 under sections 302/324, 148/149/395 PPC read with section 7 ATA 1997 police station Jauharabad District Khushab. 2.

The facts of the case have been stated by Mazhar Anwaar/PW-20 real brother of Naeemullah deceased in his statement before the learned trial court and same statement is hereby reproduced for narration of the facts: - “Naeemullah (deceased) was my younger brother whereas Hafeezullah (deceased) was his driver. Hameedullah (deceased) was also from my village. Said Rasool (pw) was “Kardar” (Manager) of Naeemullah (deceased). I know all accused of this case. Muhammad Shabbir, Ameer accused present in court and Khuda Bakhsh (PO) are real brothers. Imran Qureshi and CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 3 Muhammad Hussain accused present in court are their friends whereas Muhammad Muneer accused present in court is brother- in-law (Sala) of Muhammad Shabbir accused. On 16.02.2005 at about 01:00 pm when I alongwith Gul Hayat Bali and Gul Hayat Kandan (PWs) while moving towards Lorry Adda reached near the house of Mahboob Mastial, there came Jeep No.B- 4302/NWFP in which Naeemullah, Hafeezullah (both deceased) and Said Rasool (PW) were sitting and they were moving towards from Adda. Hafeezullah (deceased) was driving it whereas Naeemullah (deceased) was sitting on front seat. Said Rasool (PW) was on the back seat. (Jeep is parked outside the court today). When Jeep arrived near the shop of Muhammad Iqbal Lohar, it became slow due to speed breaker there and at that occasion from the shop of Muhammad Iqbal Lohar there emerged Khuda Bakhsh (PO), Muneer, Imran Qureshi, Ameer accused all armed with Kalashnikovs, Shabbir armed with G-III rifle and Muhammad Hussain @ Imran Abbas in possession of 12 bore repeater.

Said Rasool (PW) was on the back seat. (Jeep is parked outside the court today). When Jeep arrived near the shop of Muhammad Iqbal Lohar, it became slow due to speed breaker there and at that occasion from the shop of Muhammad Iqbal Lohar there emerged Khuda Bakhsh (PO), Muneer, Imran Qureshi, Ameer accused all armed with Kalashnikovs, Shabbir armed with G-III rifle and Muhammad Hussain @ Imran Abbas in possession of 12 bore repeater. They came in front of the Jeep. Shabbir accused by raising lalkara fired with G-III rifle a burst that hit on the head of Naeemullah (deceased). Imran Qureshi accused fired a burst of Kalashnikov that hit on the chest of Naeemullah deceased. Mohammad Ameer caused fired a burst that hit on the face of Hafeezullah deceased. Mohammad Hussain accused fire with repeater that hit on left flank of Said Rasool PW. Muneer accused fired a burst that hit on the fact of Naeemullah (deceased) Khuda Bakhsh PO also fired a burst that hit on the chest of Hafeezulalh deceased. There after all accused started indiscriminate firing on the jeep and fires hits on different parts of the bodies of both deceased and Said Rasool PW. At the time of escape from there towards Adda Shabbir accused had removed a Kalashinkove, its license and magazine bag of Naeemullah deceased form the Jeep. While escaping and on the way Hameedullah deceased met the accused who stated the accused had committed cruelty and he met them at a distance of about 30 feet from place of occurrence. On this the accused Shabbir, Ameer and Khuda Bakhsh PO made indiscriminate firing on Hameedullah and those hit on different parts of body of Hameedullah who died at the spot. Accused then by firing had escaped CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 4 towards Adda. All of us had witnessed this occurrence”. 3. After registration of the case, investigation started and on completion of the same report under section 173 Cr.P.C was submitted in the trial court. 4. Learned trial court after observing legal formalities provided under the Criminal Procedure Code framed the charge on 20.03.2007 against the present appellants/convicts Mohammad Shabbir, Mohammad Ameer (since died), Mohammad Imran, Mohammad Munir and Mohammad Hussain under sections 302, 324, 337-A(i), (ii), 337-F(i) (iii) (vi), 382, 395, 404, 412, 148/149 PPC read with section 7 (a) & (c) of ATA 1997 to which they pleaded not guilty and prosecution evidence was summoned. 5.

Medical evidence was furnished by Dr. Ajmal Nadeem Khan Niazi, Medical Officer, DHQ Hospital Jauharabad PW-1 who stated that 16.2.2005 he conducted the postmortem examination on the dead bodies of Malik Naeemullah, Hafeezullah and Hameedullah, and also medically examined Said Rasool injured PW-21 detail of which is as under: - “I have medically examined Said Rasool injured on 16.2.2005 at 2:10 pm and observed following injuries on his person:- 1. An entrance wound of firearm projectile 1 x 3⁄4 cm on left side of forehead. 2. An exit on center of forehead. 3. Two entrance wounds side by side each measuring 1⁄2 x 3⁄4 cm on right temporal region of skull. 4. An exit wound 3x11⁄2 cm elliptical on back of right ear (exit of injury No.3). 5. An exit wound 1⁄2 x 1⁄2 cm on inner side of left buttock. It was exit of injury No.14. 16. A grooved wound 12x3 cm on front of left thigh. 17. An entrance wound 1⁄2 x 3⁄4 cm on front of left thigh below to injury No.6. 18. An entrance wound 1⁄2 x 3⁄4 cm on front of left knee. 19. An entrance 1 x 1⁄2 cm on inner side of left knee. All injuries were kept under observation, which were caused by firearm having the duration of an hour to 1 1⁄2 hours. “On 16.02.2005 at about 04:00 pm I had conducted postmortem examination on the dead body of Malik Naeemullah son of Saifullah caste Tiwana who was brought by Police identified by Mansabdar alongwith Saifullah. It was a dead body of a middle aged man lying spine on table wearing Qameez, Dhoti, Bunian and brown colour Sweeter, Khaki Clothes. Postmortem staining not present. Rigor mortis not present. Face was damaged and only one right eye hanging from orbit. On examination I found following injuries on the body of deceased:- 1. An entrance wound 1 x3⁄4 cm on right side of fact at lower margin of right mandible. 2. An enterance wound 1 x3⁄4 cm on right side of neck below to injury No.1 3. Whole of the face missing with multiple grooves on edges of remaining skin of firearm. All the face except right eye ball faces bones and brain matter missing due to firearm injuries.

Whole of the face missing with multiple grooves on edges of remaining skin of firearm. All the face except right eye ball faces bones and brain matter missing due to firearm injuries. Scalp in upper region was missing. 4. An entrance wound 1x1⁄2 x 1 cm on right side of neck. CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 7 5. An exit wound of firearm projectile 10x4 cm on left supera clavicular fossa. 6. An entrance wound 1x3⁄4 cm on front of upper chest left side. 7. An entrance wound 1x3⁄4 cm on front of left chest, 1 cm below to injury No.6. 8. An entrance wound 1x3⁄4 cm on front of left chest, 2 cm below to injury No.7. 9. A large exit wound 12x4 cm on outer side of left shoulder. 10. An entrance wound x3⁄4 cm on front of right shoulder. 11. An exit wound 11⁄2 cm x 1 cm on back of right shoulder. 12. An entrance wound of firearm projectile on front of right loin measuring 1x3⁄4 cm. 13. (Note no injury has been mentioned against serial No.13 as after serial No.12 the witness had written serial No.14. An entrance wound 1 x3⁄4cm on front of left inguinal region. 23. An exit wound 6x3 cm on back of left. It was exit of injury No.21. 24. An entrance wound 1 1⁄2 x 1 cm on back of right chest in upper half. 25. An entrance wound 1 x 3⁄4 cm on left side of back of chest in posterior axillary line in 7th ICS. 26. An exit of injury No.24 on back of left chest in 8th ICS. It was close to mid line. 27. An entrance wound of firearm projectile 11⁄2 x 1 cm on back of right chest in 8th ICS. 28. An entrance wound of firearm projectile 11⁄2 x 2 cm on back of right lower chest. On examination of thorax right and left pleurae cavity were having blood. Two bullets were found in right chest, which were sealed and handed over to the police. Lungs, pericardium and heart were punctured through and through at many CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 8 points. Blood vessels were ruptured. Remaining organs of thorax were healthy. On abdominal examination walls, mouth, diaphraghm, stomach, small intestines and left kidney were punctured. In my opinion all injuries were ante-mortem and caused by firearm projectile. Injuries No.1 to 4, 6 to 8, 12, 15, 17, 22, 24, 25, 27 & 28 were sufficient enough to cause death by damaging vital organs and bleeding shock and death collectively as well as separately. It was sufficient enough to cause death in ordinary course of life. Time between injuries and death was immediate whereas between death and postmortem examination it was three hours. After postmortem examination the stitched dead body, postmortem report, police papers and sealed phial containing bullets were handed over to the Police. Carbon copies of postmortem report Ex.PC and sketch of injuries Ex.PC/1 and PC/2 are in my handwriting, which bear my signatures.

After postmortem examination the stitched dead body, postmortem report, police papers and sealed phial containing bullets were handed over to the Police. On the same day at about 05:00 pm I had also conducted the postmortem examination on the dead body of Hafeezullah son of Noor Muhammad aged 30 years brought by police and identified by same witnesses as earlier. It was dead body of young man lying spine on table wearing shalwar, qameez (Slati chadar), Banyan and Jercy (dark brown). Eyes were close. Mouth was injured. Rigour mortis started to develop. In my opinion all injuries were ante- mortem and caused by firearm projectile.

On abdominal examination walls, diaphragm stomach, small and large intestines were punctured and remaining organs were healthy. In my opinion all injuries were ante- mortem and caused by firearm projectile. Injuries Nos.1, 2, 9 to 11 and 31 were sufficient enough to cause death separately as well as collectively whereas other injuries associated them in causing immediate death. These injuries by CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 10 causing bleeding, shock, to vital organs damaged and sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. Time between injuries and death was immediate whereas between death and postmortem examination it was 4 hours. After postmortem examination the stitched dead body, postmortem report, police papers and sealed phial containing two bullets were handed over to police, Carbon copies of postmortem report Ex.PD and sketch of injuries Ex.PD/1 and PD/2 are in my handwriting and bear my signatures. On the same day at about 06:30 pm I had also conducted the postmortem examination on the dead body of Hameedullah son of Fazal-e-Haq who was brought by police and identified by same witnesses. It was dead body of middle aged person wearing Qameez Boski, Shalwar white, Banyan and Jercy dark brown. Eyes and mouth were close. Rigor mortis about to develop. Postmortem staining was present. On examination I had noted following injuries:- 1. An entrance wound of firearm projectile 1 x 3⁄4 cm on front of right side of chest close to nipple. 2. It would be pertinent to mention here that in order to bring a particular act within the ambit of section 7, it is to be seen as to whether the said act had created sense of fear or insecurity in public or in any section of public or community or any sect, or the occurrence was simply the result of previous enmity or personal vendetta. Since in the instant case it has been established that motive behind the incident was previously strained relations between the parties and an iota of evidence has not been brought on record to show that object was not to kill the deceased but also to strike terror or create sense of fear or insecurity in the general public or community or any sect therefore, therefore, in our view section 7 of the Anti-Terrorism Act was not attracted. In the case of Tariq Mahmood v. The State 2008 S C M R 1631, the occurrence had allegedly taken place in a Bazaar which was heavily populated and it was claimed that due to firing there was stamped in the traversers yet, since it was found that there was no evidence that the act of respondents/accused struck terror amongst the masses and the place of occurrence was not a busy populated area rather it took place on a service road, therefore, the provisions of Anti-Terrorism Act were not attracted. In the case of Muhammad Idrees and others v. The State 2008 S C M R 1544, occurrence took place on the bank of canal. It was not established on record that the act done by the accused persons had created sense of fear or insecurity in public, therefore, it was held that section 7 of the Anti- Terrorism Act was not attracted. It was not established on record that the act done by the accused persons had created sense of fear or insecurity in public, therefore, it was held that section 7 of the Anti- Terrorism Act was not attracted. In the case of Mohabbat Ali v. the State 2007 S C M R 142, the alleged incident took place because of previous enmity and private vendetta inside the fields of sugarcane and banana, about 14/15 miles away CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 22 from main road, it was held that intention of the accused was not at all to create sense of fear or insecurity or to destabilize public at large or to advance any sectarian cause and design or purpose of offence as contemplated by provisions of section 6 of Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 was not attracted. Now coming to the quantum of sentence we have noted some mitigating circumstances in favour of appellants Mohammad Shabbir, Mohammad Imran and Mohammad Munir. Firstly, we have disbelieved recoveries of Kalashnikov and G-III Rifle detail of which is mentioned in preceding paras of this judgment Secondly, Mazhar Anwaar complainant and Said Rasool PWs (eye witnesses) stated that all the accused made indiscriminate firing upon Hafeezullah and Naeemullah deceased and injured Said Rasool and further stated that Khuda Bakhsh (P.O) Muhammad Shabbir present appellant and Muhammad Ameer (since died) also made firing upon Hameedullah deceased and it is not ascertainable that which accused made fatal shot and further during indiscriminate firing made by different accused, no one can give the detail of the shots made by the accused Mohammad Shabbir, Mohammad Imran and Mohammad Munir and seat of injuries. In our considered view, injuries caused by the accused persons collectively culminated in death of the deceased persons. Reliance is placed on case CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 30 Muhammad Yaqoob and others V. The State and others (2009 SCMR 527) in which Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan has observed at pages 535 and 536 as under:- “As regards the quantum of sentence through the learned trial Judge has not inflicted normal penalty for murder on the appellants on the ground that since all the three appellants were real brothers, therefore, he did not want to inflict capital punishment on them, yet, we are afraid the reason being absurd simply and extraneous to the established principles governing the administration of criminal justice, can neither be recognized nor approved. Relationship inter se of the accused persons can hardly be a consideration for imposition of lesser penalty and it can also not be regarded as a mitigating circumstance by any stretch of imagination. However, despite that, we are not inclined to enhance the sentence inflicted on the appellants because as per record, in the instant case, firing was attributed to all the accused persons and it too, has come on record that the injuries caused by the accused persons collectively culminated in death of the deceased persons, therefore, we feel that ends of justice would be met with if sentences inflicted on the appellants are maintained as awarded by the trial Court particularly in view of the fact that the occurrence took place way back in 1999 and the accused persons have already undergone the agony of a protracted trial.

Relationship inter se of the accused persons can hardly be a consideration for imposition of lesser penalty and it can also not be regarded as a mitigating circumstance by any stretch of imagination. However, despite that, we are not inclined to enhance the sentence inflicted on the appellants because as per record, in the instant case, firing was attributed to all the accused persons and it too, has come on record that the injuries caused by the accused persons collectively culminated in death of the deceased persons, therefore, we feel that ends of justice would be met with if sentences inflicted on the appellants are maintained as awarded by the trial Court particularly in view of the fact that the occurrence took place way back in 1999 and the accused persons have already undergone the agony of a protracted trial. In this view we are fortified by the observations made in the case of Muhammad Idrees, etc. v. The State 2008 SCMR 1544. Upshot of the above discussion is that Criminal Appeal No. 103 of 2003 is partly allowed. Convictions and sentences recorded against appellants Muhammad Yaqoob and Abdul Qayyum under section 7 of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 are set aside and they are acquitted of the charges thereunder. However, rest of the convictions and sentences recorded against them under sections 302(b)/34, 324/34 and 337-A(i) PPC by the learned trial court are maintained and the sentences recorded thereunder shall run consecutively as ordered by the learned trial court. Benefit of section 382-B Cr.P.C is however, extended to the appellants. Sequel to above, Criminal appeal No. 104 of 2003 filed by the complainant for enhancement of sentences and against acquittal is dismissed”. 64. The CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 31 prosecution case is that all the three accused namely Akbar and Asghar Masih (acquitted accused) and Shera Masih appellant caused injuries to the deceased and according to the medical evidence death was cause of collective result of injuries Nos.2 and 3 but Asghar Masih was acquitted and his acquittal was not challenged either by the State or the complainant.

The injury attributed to Shera Masih was not individually found fatal to the life of deceased by the doctor who conducted postmortem upon the dead body. The CSR No.34-T of 2007 Criminal Appeal No. 534 of 2007 Appeal No. 393-2010 31 prosecution case is that all the three accused namely Akbar and Asghar Masih (acquitted accused) and Shera Masih appellant caused injuries to the deceased and according to the medical evidence death was cause of collective result of injuries Nos.2 and 3 but Asghar Masih was acquitted and his acquittal was not challenged either by the State or the complainant. Shera Masih in the light of the role assigned to him in the occurrence would stand at par to Asghar Masih and thus would not be alone responsible for causing death of Mansha Masih, therefore, the imposition of extreme penalty of death upon him in the given circumstances would not be proper and justified. We having examined the evidence in detail, have found that no exception can be taken to the conclusion of the guilt of appellants drawn by the learned Judges in the High Court except that Shera appellant would also be entitled to lesser penalty. For the foregoing reasons, we hold that conviction awarded to the appellants under section 302(a)(b)/34, 324/34 and 337-F(iii)/34, PPC was not interferable and we while maintaining the same reduce the sentence of Shera Masih, appellant No.1, from death to life imprisonment with further direction that he will also be entitled to the benefit of section 382-B, of Cr.PC.
